page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 64 dedicated physical cores built on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 34 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block read-only cached memory bandwidth reaching top-tier levels and large-block uncached read and write speeds remaining strong. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks also place this instance in the top tier, alongside high static web serving throughput. However, single-core CPU performance and LLM inference speeds for small and medium models fall into the average tier.

Due to its ARM64 architecture, the instance lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX or AMX, which may limit certain legacy workloads. However, its dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading ensure consistent compute allocation. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage, balanced memory-to-CPU ratio, and top-tier database performance makes this instance highly suitable for database hosting, high-throughput web serving, data compression tasks, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth under the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and LLM inference speeds remain average. The lack of hyperthreading and x86 instruction sets like AVX is offset by dedicated core allocation and high-speed local storage. This instance is well-suited for high-throughput database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge features 64 ARM64 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core and database performance.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores utilizing AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processors.
256.0 GB of system memory paired with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for high-throughput database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded processing.
